title: carex forsteri var. cockayneana
intro: Carex forsteri var. cockayneana, also known as mountain kaka-tahi, is a type of sedge plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. The leaves and roots of the mountain kaka-tahi plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
